2606.29118.title: An Information-Geometric Justification for Composite Coherence in Event-Based Narrative Extraction
2606.29118.paper_summary: Graph-based narrative extraction relies on a coherence function to score transitions between events, but the coherence metrics in current use are defined operationally and lack an information-theoretic foundation. We study the composite metric $C=\sqrt{A\cdot T}$, where $A$ is the angular similarity of document embeddings and $T=1-d_{\mathrm{JS}}$ is a topic proximity from the Jensen-Shannon distance of soft memberships, and give it an information-geometric reading together with an axiomatic characterization of the geometric-mean combinator. On the product manifold $\mathbb{S}^{d-1}\timesΔ^{K-1}$, the negative log-coherence decomposes additively into an angular and a topic cost. Because the Riemannian metric tensor induced by the Jensen-Shannon distance on the simplex is proportional to the Fisher information matrix, the topic component is locally consistent with the Fisher-Rao metric singled out by Chentsov's theorem. Within the compensability spectrum of combinators, the geometric mean is the unique one consistent with four natural axioms (a boundary/veto condition, symmetry, log-additivity, normalization), and the construction motivates a proper product metric $d_\times$. Experiments on four corpora, three embedding families, and three topic models are consistent with the framework: the Fisher identity holds ($R\ge0.99$), the geometric mean tracks $d_\times$ closely ($ρ=0.999$), and a downstream LLM-as-judge check finds it is not dominated by any alternative combinator or single-channel baseline. Sweeping the spectrum, the bottleneck-coherence gap between extracted and random storylines splits into a symmetric component, maximized at the geometric mean across five corpora, and a displacement term; a cross-modal image-narrative case study reproduces the effect. These results justify the composite coherence metric and articulate when the geometric mean is the natural choice.
2606.29118.paper_summary_zh: 圖基敘事提取依賴於一致性函數來評分事件之間的轉換，但目前使用的一致性度量是操作性定義的，缺乏信息理論基礎。我們研究了複合度量 $C=\sqrt{A\cdot T}$，其中 $A$ 是文檔嵌入的角度相似性，而 $T=1-d_{\mathrm{JS}}$ 是來自軟成員的詹森-香農距離的主題接近度，並給它一個信息幾何的解讀，連同幾何平均組合子的公理特徵。在乘積流形 $\mathbb{S}^{d-1}\timesΔ^{K-1}$ 上，負對數一致性可加性地分解為角度成本和主題成本。由詹森-香農距離在單純形上誘導的黎曼度量張量與費舍爾信息矩陣成正比，因此主題組件在局部上與由 Chentsov 定理突出的費舍爾-拉奧度量一致。在組合子的可補償性光譜中，幾何平均是唯一與四個自然公理（邊界/否決條件、對稱性、對數可加性、正規化）一致的，這一構造激勵了一個適當的乘積度量 $d_\times$。對四個語料庫、三個嵌入系列和三個主題模型的實驗與該框架一致：費舍爾恆等式成立 ($R\ge0.99$)，幾何平均與 $d_\times$ 緊密跟蹤 ($ρ=0.999$)，而下游 LLM-as-judge 檢查發現它並未被任何替代組合子或單通道基線所主導。掃描光譜，提取的故事情節與隨機故事情節之間的瓶頸一致性差距分解為一個對稱組件，在五個語料庫中在幾何平均時達到最大，以及一個位移項；一個跨模態圖像-敘事案例研究重現了這一效果。這些結果證實了複合一致性度量，並闡明了何時幾何平均是自然的選擇。
2606.29118.author: Brian Keith-Norambuena et.al.
